description: Federated driver-data sources, keyed by a short stable source id. OpenISD links to driver libraries wherever their owners maintain them, instead of copying the files. The in-app driver browser reads this map (and you can paste any GitHub repo ad hoc). A driver's unique identity is its source key + its path within that source — never its display name, which can repeat. Add a source by opening a PR that adds a keyed entry here; the key is the drivers/ folder name for bundled sources, or a short slug for federated ones.

demos.name: OpenISD demos
demos.description: Generic demo drivers shipped with OpenISD (a 6.5" woofer and a 1" tweeter) — not real products; representative values for trying the tool. The woofer is the app's default driver.

matt.name: Matt (mtg90) / AVS Forum
matt.description: ~430 drivers from the mtg90 AVS Forum collection. See drivers/matt/meta.json for provenance.

loudspeakerdatabase.name: loudspeakerdatabase
loudspeakerdatabase.description: Drivers derived from loudspeakerdatabase.com, a third-party aggregator — not independently re-verified against any manufacturer source. Maintained by the winisd_tools pipeline (private).
loudspeakerdatabase.license: No license — no warranty on accuracy. See loudspeakerdatabase.com for its own terms.

dayton-audio.name: Dayton Audio
dayton-audio.description: Drivers derived from the daytonaudio.com product page specification table. Maintained by the winisd_tools pipeline (private). Refresh with: python scrape_dayton.py
dayton-audio.license: No license — no warranty on accuracy. Values are derived from daytonaudio.com's public product pages.

sb-acoustics.name: SB Acoustics
sb-acoustics.description: 201 drivers derived from the manufacturer's datasheet PDF (primary source), with the sbacoustics.com product page filling any gaps. Covers SB Acoustics and Satori lines — woofers, midrange, tweeters. PDF datasheets linked in _meta.json. All quality=M (not human-verified). Maintained by the winisd_tools pipeline (private). Refresh with: python scrapers/scrape_sbacoustics.py
sb-acoustics.license: No license — no warranty on accuracy. Values are derived from public SB Acoustics datasheets and product pages.

scan-speak.name: Scan-Speak
scan-speak.description: 114 drivers derived from the manufacturer's datasheet PDF (primary source), with the scan-speak.dk product page filling any gaps — woofers, midrange, tweeters, and Revelator/Ellipticor lines. PDF datasheets and ZIP measurement files (SPL + impedance) downloaded locally. Quality=M (not human-verified). Maintained by the winisd_tools pipeline (private). Refresh with: python scrapers/scrape_scanspeak.py --refresh
scan-speak.license: No license — no warranty on accuracy. Values are derived from public Scan-Speak datasheets and product pages.

soundimports.name: SoundImports
soundimports.description: Drivers derived from the soundimports.eu product page specification table. Maintained by the winisd_tools pipeline (private). Refresh with: python scrapers/scrape_soundimports.py
soundimports.license: No license — no warranty on accuracy. Values are derived from soundimports.eu's public product pages.

wavecor.name: Wavecor
wavecor.description: 73 drivers derived from the wavecor.com product page specification table — woofers, subwoofers, and full-range drivers (WF/SW/FR series). Datasheet PDF and SPL/impedance TXT measurement files also downloaded and linked, not just the WDR values. Quality=M (not human-verified). Maintained by the winisd_tools pipeline (private). Refresh with: python scrapers/scrape_wavecor.py --refresh
wavecor.license: No license — no warranty on accuracy. Values are derived from public Wavecor product pages and datasheets.

parts-express.name: Parts Express woofer guide
parts-express.description: 1673 drivers whose T/S values are derived from the manufacturer's datasheet PDF (primary), falling back to the product page's own T/S table. The Parts Express product API (https://www.parts-express.com/api/items?q={sku}&fieldset=details) is used only to discover which drivers exist — its own bare spec fields are not used as values. Covers woofers, subwoofers, tweeters, midrange/full-range, passive radiators, planar/ribbon, car audio, and pro audio drivers. Brands: Aurum Cantus, B&C Speakers, Beyma, Celestion, Ciare, CSS, Dayton Audio, EPIQUE, Eminence Speaker, FaitalPRO, Goldwood, GRS, HiVi, JBL Professional, Lavoce, Morel, Peerless by Tymphany, PRV Audio, Selenium, Tang Band, Tectonic, Timpano Audio, Visaton, Wavecor and others. See meta.json for quality flags. Maintained by the winisd_tools pipeline (private). Refresh with: python scrapers/scrape_pe.py
parts-express.license: No license — no warranty on accuracy. Values are derived from public manufacturer datasheets and product pages; parts-express.com is used only to discover which drivers exist.

winisd.name: WinISD
winisd.description: 1121 driver files from the original WinISD distribution (circa 2006 or earlier). Quality=L (obsolete, unchecked). Most drivers are likely subsumed by more recent sources.
